findings.description: The PDF uses a two-column layout for Receipts and Disbursements (FY 2020-2021 on the left, Duration of Program on the right). The HTML converts this into a single four-column table, which changes the visual grouping from side-by-side independent sections to a merged table structure.
findings.evidence: PDF shows two visually separate sections with their own headers ('Receipts:' and 'Disbursements:' appearing twice each). HTML merges them into one wide table with columns: FY 2020-2021 Category, Amount, Duration of Program Category, Amount.

findings.description: The Fund Balance and Encumbered in Contracts lines are presented as a simple right-aligned summary in the PDF, but rendered as a separate table in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Fund Balance as of 3/31/2021: $ 13,054,308.66' with bold formatting inline. HTML puts these into a Description/Amount table.

findings.description: Dash/hyphen values for zero or empty amounts in the PDF (e.g., Rebate: $ -, Interest: $ -) are rendered as '$ -' in the HTML, which is acceptable, but the underline formatting on section headers like 'Receipts:' and 'Disbursements:' is not preserved in the HTML table.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Receipts:' and 'Disbursements:' with underline decoration. HTML shows them as plain text rows in the table without underline styling.

findings.description: Reading order is functionally preserved. The DSCA Site Statistics section appears after the financial data in both versions, and the fund balance summary appears between them in both.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML present: financial data → fund balance summary → site statistics.

findings.description: HTML table headers include fabricated column names not present in the PDF
findings.evidence: The HTML adds column headers like 'FY 2020-2021 (through 3/31/21) Category', 'FY 2020-2021 (through 3/31/21) Amount', 'Description', 'Amount', 'Statistic', 'Value' which are not explicitly in the PDF but are reasonable structural labels for the table data. These are organizational headers rather than fabricated content.

findings.description: The category labels are duplicated across columns 1 and 3 of the main table. In the PDF, each side has its own set of line items, but the labels are identical. The HTML repeats the label text in both column 1 and column 3, which while matching the PDF layout, creates redundancy in the table structure.
findings.evidence: E.g., 'Solvent Tax Revenue:' appears in both column 1 and column 3, 'Sales Tax Revenue:' appears in both columns, etc. This mirrors the PDF's two-column layout, so this is actually faithful reproduction.

findings.description: There are two nested <main> elements in the HTML, which is a structural issue but does not affect content fidelity.
findings.evidence: The HTML has <main> inside <body> and another <main> inside the first <main>.
